title: Shrink LTX text-encoder: GGUF Gemma (Q4/Q5) + abliterated LoRA vs the 14.5GB fp8 heretic — the real LTX speed lever
description:
GOAL: make LTX-2.3 generations FASTER by shrinking the resident text-encoder footprint. LTX is reputed FASTER than Wan, yet OUR LTX is ~2x SLOWER than our Wan (Pod cold 4:29 vs 2:11, warm 22-25s vs 9-14s) and up to ~20x slower when system RAM can't hold the working set. Our slowness is LOAD/RAM-bound, NOT compute (sampler steps are FAST: 1.45 s/it Pod). The working set ~40-45GB = ~15GB Q8 transformer + ~14.5GB fp8 Gemma encoder + ~4GB VAEs/projection + overhead. GEMMA IS HALF THE FOOTPRINT and the only piece not yet shrunk -> highest-leverage speed+RAM win available. Shrinking it ~7GB pulls the working set under the 60GB-RAM ceiling -> kills the low-RAM thrash (MPI-160) AND speeds cold load everywhere.

KEY UNLOCK (from the OFFICIAL LTX-2.3 t2v ComfyUI workflow, user-provided 2026-06-30): the 'heretic' uncensoring is NOT baked into Gemma — the official workflow uses STOCK gemma + a separate abliterated LoRA (`gemma-3-12b-it-abliterated_lora_rank64_bf16.safetensors`). So we are NOT locked to the rare `anongecko/gemma-3-12b-it-heretic` — we can use ANY stock gemma-3-12b GGUF + the LoRA to restore the same behavior. This removes the 'no GGUF of the heretic variant' blocker. ALSO: the official t2v ships `gemma_3_12B_it_fp4_mixed.safetensors` (fp4!) — so Lightricks themselves ship fp4 Gemma, which CONTRADICTS our terse 'fp4 hurts' note (docs/builder/research/model-set.md:13, a one-liner with no documented A/B; likely a different/untuned fp4). GGUF Q-quant (int+per-block-scale) should hold better than flat fp4 anyway — same reason Q8 transformer beat fp8.

DOWNLOAD (bartowski, confirmed 2026-06-30) — test BOTH:
  Q4_K_M (7.3GB, ~ the fp4 size): https://huggingface.co/bartowski/google_gemma-3-12b-it-GGUF/resolve/main/google_gemma-3-12b-it-Q4_K_M.gguf
  Q5_K_M (8.44GB, more quality headroom): https://huggingface.co/bartowski/google_gemma-3-12b-it-GGUF/resolve/main/google_gemma-3-12b-it-Q5_K_M.gguf
  + abliterated LoRA: gemma-3-12b-it-abliterated_lora_rank64_bf16.safetensors (from the official workflow / civitai/HF — find exact source).
  IGNORE the repo's mmproj-*.gguf files — those are VISION projection (multimodal image input); LTX uses Gemma as a TEXT encoder only.

WORKFLOW WIRING (the test): swap the stock `DualCLIPLoader` -> city96 `DualCLIPLoader (GGUF)`; clip_name1 = the GGUF gemma; clip_name2 = ltx-2.3_text_projection_bf16.safetensors (UNCHANGED — mixed GGUF clip1 + bf16 clip2; city96 docs say the loader handles both gguf AND safetensors/bin); type = ltxv; add the abliterated LoRA on the CLIP path (MpiLoraModelClip or a clip-lora loader at the strength the official workflow uses). 

LOAD-FEASIBILITY (answers itself on first load — likely all YES): (1) does DualCLIPLoader (GGUF) expose `ltxv` in its type dropdown? VERY LIKELY YES — city96's loader INHERITS the type enum from the stock node (`type: base['required']['type']` where base = nodes.DualCLIPLoader.INPUT_TYPES()), so it has the exact same type list as the stock loader we use now (which has ltxv). (2) does it load a GGUF clip1 + bf16 safetensors clip2 mixed? LIKELY YES per city96 docs ('the loader can handle both types - gguf and regular safetensors/bin'). (3) does the abliterated LoRA apply cleanly on a GGUF-quant base? unverified.

A/B (the decider): stock-gemma-GGUF(Q4, then Q5) + abliterated-LoRA  VS  current heretic-fp8. Measure: (a) QUALITY — prompt adherence + face/eyes/teeth (the exact things 'fp4 hurts' supposedly broke) + general output; (b) SPEED/RAM — working-set size, cold load time, warm time, and behavior on a LOW-RAM (60GB) Pod where the current set thrashes. Q4 first (biggest win, matches fp4 size); if it hurts, Q5; if Q5 holds, ship Q5; if both hold, ship Q4.

IMPORTANT — DO NOT gate the decision on PROVING the abliterated LoRA works (user, 2026-06-30): prior testing FAILED to find prompts that show the LoRA's effect (tried gore + NSFW, saw little/no difference vs without). The team's only firm finding was specialized-heretic-Gemma >= LoRA, but the diff was subtle/un-pin-downable. So the LoRA's contribution is UNTESTABLE for us right now. Therefore: INCLUDE the LoRA as a SHIP-IT HEDGE, NOT a test gate. Rationale: (1) it's what the official workflow uses = proven-compatible wiring; (2) moving OFF the specialized heretic Gemma, we want SOME uncensor-restore insurance; (3) cheap (~0.5GB); (4) we can't prove it works but also couldn't prove it doesn't. The REAL test = does stock-GGUF-Gemma (with the LoRA riding along) MATCH fp8-heretic on quality while being ~7GB lighter + faster. Do NOT try to isolate the LoRA's effect — just keep it in as the safe default. People online report it helps on certain (basic) prompts; good enough to keep, not worth blocking on.

IF IT HOLDS: LTX gets ~7GB lighter -> faster cold load everywhere + the RAM-floor problem (MPI-160) largely dissolves + restores LTX's 'faster than Wan' credibility. Then wire it as a dep (engine split? — Gemma is shared local+Pod, so likely NOT engine-tagged, just a straight swap fp8->GGUF for BOTH engines; confirm local high-res quality too). Re-host to R2 per supply-chain rule.

This is a BUILDER-POD authoring + research task (docs/builder/). BRANCH: RunPod. Relates: MPI-157 (GGUF transformer split), MPI-160 (RAM-floor), the perf reframe in pod-perf-investigation.md.
status_note: CLOSED as won't-do by user 2026-07-02. Gemma text-encoder delivery is DECIDED: fp4_mixed shared clip (ltx23-gemma-clip, gemma_3_12B_it_fp4_mixed.safetensors 9.45GB, on R2, SHARED across every engine + tier). We are NOT pursuing bf16 OR GGUF for this clip — that whole axis is dropped. This card's premise (shrink Gemma via GGUF) is therefore dead. The 'per-VRAM-tier Gemma re-decide' the old note deferred to is CANCELLED — fp4-shared is the answer for all tiers. Locked finding (Q4 GGUF loads + zero visible quality diff) preserved below for history only; NOT going to be shipped. R2 ORPHANS NOW DEAD — safe to GC (needs user go, live op): cubric-models Q4 GGUF Gemma 7.3GB sha fc57f67e + heretic-abliterated LoRA 599MB sha caa438a3 (recorded docs/builder/04-add-models.md). ── ORIGINAL PARK NOTE (history) ── PARKED 2026-06-30, NOT cancelled — re-work pending, not reversal. The Detailer-LoRA + LoRA-merge + low/balanced/high VRAM-tier restructure will RE-CHANGE this work (tiers remove the bf16-local/GGUF-Pod asymmetry, so Gemma delivery gets re-decided per tier, not re-reverted). LOCKED FINDING (carry forward, do NOT re-test): Q4_K_M GGUF Gemma (bartowski) = ZERO visible quality diff vs 14.5GB fp8 heretic (user A/B, local), loads+gens via DualCLIPLoaderGGUF (mixed gguf-clip1 + bf16-projection-clip2, type=ltxv) → ~7GB-lighter encoder PROVEN viable. NO CODE SHIPPED — dependencies.js/models.js/workflow JSONs untouched (inspected only). RE-WORK WHEN TIERS LAND: (1) decide Q4-GGUF vs fp8 Gemma PER VRAM TIER (low/balanced/high), not per engine; (2) the abliterated/Detailer LoRAs move toward baked MERGE into weights (other-models pattern) instead of runtime loaders; (3) re-host: if Gemma GGUF survives the merge decision, its R2 copy becomes a real dep — else GC. R2 ORPHANS PENDING GC (cubric-models, recorded in docs/builder/04-add-models.md): Q4 GGUF 7.3GB sha fc57f67e + heretic-abliterated LoRA 599MB sha caa438a3 — keep until the tier restructure decides delivery. See events.jsonl 2026-06-30 note.
